Ad Text:
About the role

The Support Systems Engineer is a key member of the Support System Team, operating within the broader engineering function. This role combines Systems Engineering and Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) Engineering and supports delivery of end-to-end Support System outcomes across defence platforms.

Working closely with the Support System Lead, you will contribute across the full support system scope including requirements development, ILS, support facilities and training systems.

You will act as a technical integrator, supporting communication across engineering, stakeholders, and the customer, while stepping in across the Support System scope as priorities demand.

We work in a fast-paced, demanding, challenging as well as time sensitive environment; we're diverse, global, resilient, adaptable and agile, therefore the ability to solve problems is critical.

Along with satisfaction and rewards that comes with working for a global defence company, you can expect a generous salary, a range of outstanding benefits and an inclusive working environment.

This position also offers flexible working options and a 9-day fortnight.

As part of your specific responsibilities, you will:

Support System Engineering

Contribute to all elements of the Support System scope, including:
Requirements development through translation of stakeholder and customer needs
Definition and support of support facilities, including design intent, usage, and constraints
Development of Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) activities and documentation
Development of the training strategy
Support mandated system reviews and contribute to review preparation and execution.

Systems Engineering & ILS Integration

Apply systems engineering principles across the support system lifecycle
Review Support System documentation and engineering artefacts
Provide advice and guidance to internal and external stakeholders on Support System scope
Participate in working groups and technical reviews at various levels.

Stakeholder & Customer Engagement

Assist the Support System Lead with task coordination and stakeholder communication
Attend and actively contribute to customer meetings and solution development
Support the preparation of future Support System tasking statements
Contribute to closure of working group actions with key stakeholders.
About you

Your skills and experience will include:

Essential

Experience across the systems engineering lifecycle, with a strong support system and/or ILS focus
Strong written and verbal communication skills, including development of high-quality technical artefacts
Proven experience working with diverse internal and external stakeholders to achieve shared outcomes
Ability to work autonomously, manage priorities, and deliver outputs to time, cost, and quality.

Desirable

Knowledge or experience in one or more C4ISREW or Combat System Warfare domains (AAW, ASW, ASuW)
Experience conducting Logistic Support Analysis (LSA) within the Australian Defence environment
Experience in Defence or a comparable technical industry
Project management experience.

Qualification:

Tertiary qualification in Engineering or a relevant discipline, or equivalent Defence industry experience.

Personal Attributes

Strong understanding of support system principles
Structured problem-solver with an outcome-focused mindset
Collaborative, flexible, and approachable team player
Confident communicator able to influence across teams and stakeholders
Proactive self-starter with strong self-management skills
Curious and adaptable, with a desire to explore new opportunities and solutions.

Applicants must be Australian citizens and eligible to obtain and maintain a NV1 defence clearance. Information including past and present nationality and citizenship may be requested to check eligibility for access to materials controlled under various export control regimes, including ITAR.

About us

Saab Australia is a defence, security and cyber solutions provider, specialising in the development and integration of major defence and security systems. For over 30 years in Australia, we have built a reputation for delivering complex systems integration projects that provide proven and advanced capabilities to keep us ahead of today's challenges.

From combat, communications, security and underwater systems, to mixed reality, cyber security and deployable health solutions, our extensive portfolio of products and services provide high-technology solutions for defence forces and civil industries across the globe.

Apply today to join our crew and deliver solutions with purpose.

What you will be part of

Explore a wealth of possibilities. Take on challenges, create smart inventions, and grow beyond. This is a place for curious minds, brave pioneers, and everyone in between. Together, we achieve the extraordinary, each bringing our unique perspectives. Your part matters.

Saab is a leading defence and security company with an enduring mission, to help nations keep their people and society safe. Empowered by its 26,000 talented people, Saab constantly pushes the boundaries of technology to create a safer and more sustainable world.

Saab designs, manufactures and maintains advanced systems in aeronautics, weapons, command and control, sensors and underwater systems. Saab is headquartered in Sweden. It has major operations all over the world and is part of the domestic defence capability of several nations. Read more about us here.

Culture and benefits

We employ over 1000 Australians and are committed to fostering a culture of excellence and enriching the employee experience.

By joining Saab, you'll be a part of a collaborative and supportive organisation, where your professional growth and work-life balance are emphasised. You'll work within an innovative and vibrant workplace, while having the opportunity to develop a long-term, fulfilling career.

As a valued team member of Saab Australia, you'll access a variety of perks and benefits to support you and your family including:

6 weeks paid leave each year
14% Superannuation
Free Income Protection Insurance
Flexible working options
Access to the Saab shares matching scheme
Access to MySaab - our online store offering everything from cash back at hundreds of stores to discounts from exclusive partners.

At Flight Centre Travel Group (FCTG) our purpose is to 'open up the world for those who want to see'. Every day, we give people all around the world the opportunity to experience something amazing – travel!  


We're building the AI systems that power smarter decisions across FCTG's global Supply business, and we're looking for an AI Engineer to join our Brisbane-based Data Science & AI team.

This is a hands-on engineering role working across the full AI lifecycle, from building and testing AI components to deploying production ML pipelines and agentic workflows. You'll work closely with Data Scientists (who hand validated models to you), Data Engineers, Architects, and Infrastructure Engineers to build AI systems that are reliable, observable, and built to scale.

We care about the depth and quality of your experience, not the number of years on a CV. Exceptional graduates and seasoned practitioners are equally welcome. Whether you've spent two years going very deep or moved across a range of problems, apply and show us what you've actually done! What matters is whether you can think carefully, work rigorously, and build things that hold up.

Day to day:

Designing, building, and deploying AI workflows, including classical ML pipelines and agentic systems, in production environments
Implementing and maintaining MLOps pipelines including CI/CD, model registry, monitoring, and automated evaluation
Writing clean, well-documented Python code for AI system components, data processing, and API integration
Supporting observability across ML and AI systems, logging, latency tracking, and evaluation metrics
Containerising and deploying AI workloads using Docker and Kubernetes
Collaborating with Data Scientists, Data Engineers, Architects, and Infrastructure Engineers to integrate AI components into broader technology platforms
Participating in code reviews, design discussions, and team knowledge-sharing sessions


You'll be perfect for the role if you have:

A tertiary qualification in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science, Mathematics, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience
Solid Python programming skills and a working understanding of core software engineering principles
Exposure to machine learning, classical methods like supervised and unsupervised learning, and/or AI application development, whether through work, study, or personal projects
Familiarity with cloud platforms (Azure preferred) and/or containerisation tools such as Docker and Kubernetes
A working understanding of CI/CD concepts and version control using Git
Strong communication and collaboration skills when working with cross-functional teams


You'll earn brownie points with:

Hands-on experience with Azure services or Databricks
Experience building or deploying ML models in a production setting, regardless of whether that's classical ML or agentic systems
Experience building data engineering pipelines using PySpark and Databricks
Relevant Azure certifications (e.g. Azure AI Engineer Associate, Azure Data Scientist Associate)
Experience with front-end technologies (e.g. TypeScript, React) or infrastructure tooling (e.g. Terraform, Bicep)
Open-source contributions, personal GitHub projects, or published technical work

Job Title: Staff Engineer Chapter Lead – Generative AI
Ad Text:
Do work that matters:

Help build tomorrow’s bank today by delivering cutting-edge Generative AI that transforms how millions of customers and colleagues experience financial services. As a Staff Software Engineer – Chapter Lead – Generative AI, you’ll drive the AI and strategic technology roadmap, shaping how GenAI is applied at scale across CommSec. This is a hands-on leadership role where you’ll build production-grade capabilities, support groups and functions in unlocking AI value, and lead a team of highly specialised senior engineers through CommSec’s Generative AI journey.

You’ll set enterprise standards with strong foundations in MLOps, evaluation, observability, and security, while fostering a culture of inclusion, ownership, and technical excellence. The impact is real, accelerating your career while delivering meaningful innovation, improving customer outcomes, and helping shape Australia’s future economy. 

This role is for a technical leader who is hands‑on in the code, builds real solutions, solves complex problems, and leads a high‑performing team of senior engineers.




See yourself in our team:  

Within CommSec, the CommSec Technology Platform Crew sits within the broader CommSec Technology division, part of the Business Banking Technology domain, operating at the intersection of trading, data, and advanced AI. The team builds intelligent systems that directly shape how Australians invest and manage wealth, delivering production-grade AI, Generative AI, and agentic solutions that are deeply embedded into the CommSec ecosystem.

Their work spans across Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), agentic workflows, and real-time integrations with trading and customer platforms. They apply machine learning, NLP, and advanced analytics to solve complex, real-world problems, from generating market insights to automating sophisticated workflows.

With a strong engineering discipline, the team drives CI/CD, infrastructure as code, observability, and security-first practices to ensure systems are reliable, scalable, and production-ready, shaping how Generative AI is applied in a high-impact, regulated environment.




We’re interested in hearing from people who: 

Build and scale production-grade GenAI platforms (LLMs, RAG, agentic workflows) by creating reusable frameworks and integrating them into existing enterprise systems, while leading engineers to make pragmatic architecture decisions and align delivery with real-world business problems

Translate advanced ML and NLP capabilities into deployable solutions by guiding teams through design trade-offs (latency, cost, accuracy), ensuring engineers understand both the underlying models and how they operate in production environments

Establish end-to-end AI quality and safety practices (evaluation frameworks, LLM observability, bias/risk controls) and lead teams to adopt data-driven validation approaches, embedding accountability and engineering rigor in day-to-day development

Drive strong DevSecOps maturity (CI/CD, IaC, automated testing, monitoring, cost optimisation) by setting engineering standards, enabling teams to ship reliably at scale, and coaching them on production ownership including on-call and incident response

Champion automation-first engineering culture across the AI lifecycle, reducing manual overhead while empowering teams to focus on higher-value problem solving and continuous improvement

Lead, mentor, and grow high-performing engineering teams by fostering autonomy, psychological safety, and constructive challenge, ensuring individuals build deep expertise in both AI technologies and scalable system design

Communicate effectively with senior stakeholders by translating complex AI concepts into clear trade-offs, guiding teams to balance innovation with risk, and aligning technical execution with strategic business outcomes




Tech skills 

We don't expect you to know everything, but experience in several of the following will set you up for success:

Programming Language: Python

AI/ML Frameworks: LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, AutoGen

Generative AI: Agentic frameworks, RAG, Model Evaluation

Vector Databases: Pinecone, Weaviate, Opensearch

ML Engineering: Model Training/Inference Pipeline/Orchestration development

MLOps: Model lifecycle management, Model Serving/Performance Observability

Cloud Platform: AWS services

Infrastructure as Code: CloudFormation, CDK, Terraform

Containerisation: Docker, Kubernetes

CI/CD: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins

AI Evaluation: Evaluation frameworks from traditional Machine Learning model to GenAI workflow/objects

Monitoring: Evidently AI, Datadog

Observability & Reliability Engineering: Observe, Prometheus, Grafana, PagerDuty, CloudWatch

Security: AI/ML system security

Governance: AI governance frameworks

Location: Bayside & South Eastern Suburbs, Melbourne VIC (Hybrid)

Position Description:


We are seeking a versatile Senior Data Scientist who can work across applied analytics and machine learning domains, including computer vision, statistical analysis, and policy‑relevant insight generation. This role is ideal for a data scientist who enjoys balancing exploratory analysis, model development, and clear communication of findings to technical and non‑technical audiences.
You will work with multidisciplinary teams including data engineers, BI developers, architects, and business stakeholders. You will focus on analysis, modeling, experimentation, and insight generation.
This position supports a wide range of analytics and AI initiatives such as computer vision prototyping, urban and regulatory analytics, housing and economic trend analysis, and other data‑driven decision‑support projects.

Your future duties and responsibilities:


. Conduct statistical, time‑series, and trend analysis to identify patterns, anomalies, changes over time, and emerging signals.
. Develop clear, reproducible analytical methods, KPIs, and metrics used in dashboards and recurring reports.
. Translate analytical outcomes into recommendations, narratives, and visual summaries for stakeholders.
. Support compliance, policy, planning, operational, or performance‑monitoring use cases with data‑driven insights.
. Collaborate with BI developers to ensure analytical logic is accurately reflected in dashboards and reports.

Computer Vision & Machine Learning

. Design and run computer vision experiments (classification, detection, segmentation) using real‑world imagery.
. Handle preprocessing challenges such as variable image quality, occlusions, inconsistent lighting, and diverse viewpoints.
. Develop evaluation methods, dataset documentation, and experiment reproducibility practices.
. Work within modern ML platforms to manage models, experiment tracking, and model artifacts.
. Participate in human‑in‑the‑loop evaluation workflows to validate model performance and support iterative improvement.

Collaboration & Governance

. Work within Azure ML and related Azure services.
. Partner with data engineers, architects, and SMEs to ensure analytical readiness and data quality.
. Produce well‑structured documentation: model cards, metric definitions, analytical notes, and reproducible notebooks.
. Adhere to privacy, security, and Responsible AI practices when handling sensitive or identifiable data.
. Communicate results clearly to diverse technical and non‑technical audiences.

Required qualifications to be successful in this role:


. Bachelor's degree in a quantitative or technical discipline is required.
. Strong analytical reasoning skills, including the ability to translate ambiguous business or policy questions into structured analytical or ML approaches.
. Experience applying modern AI techniques (e.g., embeddings, semantic search, or foundation‑model‑powered analysis) in practical, production‑minded settings.
. Hands‑on experience evaluating or integrating pre‑trained models into analytics or ML workflows using Azure AI services.
. Experience with Azure AI Foundry or similar foundation‑model experimentation environments.
. Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively with data engineers, BI developers, architects, and domain experts in multidisciplinary teams.
. Experience designing and evaluating experiments, including defining metrics, baselines, sampling strategies, and reproducible evaluation procedures.
. Understanding of Responsible AI principles, risk mitigation, bias evaluation, and explainability best practices.
. 5+ years of experience in applied data science or analytics, producing measurable business or policy impacts.
. Strong Python skills (pandas, numpy, scikit‑learn; plus PyTorch or TensorFlow for ML/CV).
. Strong SQL skills for querying and analytical exploration.
. Ability to communicate complex technical concepts in clear, accessible language.
. Experience working in environments with privacy, security, or auditability requirements.

Preferred Qualifications

. Master's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics, or a related field is preferred
. Experience with cloud‑based ML environments or ML lifecycle tools.
. Experience with geospatial data, coordinate systems, or spatial analysis.
. Familiarity with annotation workflows, labeling QA, or human‑in‑the‑loop processes.
. Background in impact analysis, behavioral pattern detection, or compliance analytics.
. Knowledge of Responsible AI principles and data‑ethics practices.

You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.

Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.

Reapit – Who are we?

Reapit is the original, end-to-end business technology provider for estate agencies of all sizes. We’ve been helping sales and lettings agents to build relationships and grow their businesses for more than 25 years. Our technology connects property professionals in Europe, the Middle East, Australia, and New Zealand with buyers, sellers, tenants and landlords to power the relationships that change lives.


In Australia, Reapit stands as the preferred technology choice among the nation's leading estate agents and agencies. Tailored to the unique demands of the Australian property market, Reapit provides successful leaders with unparalleled tools across sales, property management, client relations, and data analytics, reinforcing their position at the pinnacle of real estate excellence.

What you’ll be doing

Reporting to the Global Head of Data you’ll be involved in the following;

Agentic Platform Development

Design and build scalable AI agent architecture to support autonomous task execution and workflow automation

Develop self-learning systems that adapt and improve performance based on customer transaction patterns and user interactions

Implement robust patterns and practices for multi-agent and AI augmented RPA workflows

Ensure security with customer data isolation and regulatory compliance

AI-Powered Automation & Insights

Create intelligent systems for customer data analysis, market trend identification, and anomaly detection

Build automated workflows for lead qualification, maintenance management and many other agency tasks

Platform Engineering & Integration

Architect and maintain production-ready AI services using TypeScript/JavaScript, .NET, and/or Python on AWS infrastructure & services

Integrate AI capabilities with existing Reapit systems and third-party APIs while ensuring data consistency, performance and cost efficiency.

Implement monitoring, logging, and observability solutions for AI model performance and agent behaviour tracking

Technical Leadership & Innovation

Evaluate and integrate cutting-edge AI platforms, frameworks, and tooling to maintain competitive advantage

Collaborate with product and engineering teams to translate business requirements into technical AI solutions

Mentor wider engineers and establish best practices for AI development, testing, and deployment

Who we're looking for

At Reapit, we prioritise hiring individuals who share our values and possess the right attitudes and behaviours for success. Whilst some of the listed requirements may be important, don’t worry if you don’t meet all of them, we’d still like to hear from you.

5+ years of software engineering experience with strong proficiency in TypeScript/JavaScript, .NET, and/or Python

3+ years of hands-on experience building and deploying AI/ML systems for enterprise-grade solutions, preferably in a SaaS or platform environment.

Deep expertise with major AI platforms and frameworks (OpenAI, Anthropic, LangChain, TensorFlow, PyTorch)

Proven experience with AWS services including Lambda, ECS/EKS, S3, CloudFormation

Strong database skills with MySQL and experience with data pipeline development

Experience building autonomous agents, multi-agent systems, or intelligent workflow automation, as well as traditional RPA

Solid understanding of software architecture patterns, API design, and system integration principles

Bachelor's degree in computer science, AI/ML, or related technical field

Relevant AWS Certification in core or speciality paths

Knowledge of natural language processing, computer vision, or recommendation systems

Familiarity with MLOps practices, evaluation, and A/B testing frameworks

Experience with microservices architecture and event-driven systems

Understanding of data privacy regulations and security best practices for AI systems

Contributions to open-source projects in relevant domains

What your impact and success looks like

We expect your success and impact in the early stages of your career with us to look something like this:

Within 1 month:

You’ll be discovering how to, and actively integrate AI functionality into simple features in our products

Participating with the wider team, determining AI strategy and tooling

Within 3 months:

You will feel comfortable with the basics of the domain and Reapit’s products & services.

You will be able to demonstrate an understanding of the core areas of the technology stack.

You will be independently contributing new ideas to product and processes.

You will be contributing more advanced agentic features to Reapit products, and helping hone AI strategy from initial feature feedback

Within 6 months:

You will be able to work independently due to your continually growing understanding of our products and solutions.

Your contributions will be helping to shape the backlog and future direction of the wider products, where AI exists at its core.

You’ll be an advocate for the AI strategy throughout the business, guiding the wider teams adoption

We operate a Flexible Working Policy and we would like for you to work from our Brisbane or Sydney office, occasionally making an in-person appearance but otherwise able to work-from-home.

Don't tick all the boxes? Neither do we

We care about our industry and want it to become a more inclusive and diverse place to work. So, we’re driven by hiring not only by experience and relevance for the role but by sharing our values and the right attitudes and behaviours for success. We are committed to Equal Employment Opportunity through attracting and retaining a complementary team of employees and building an inclusive environment for all. We feel we have an empowering environment where everyone is supported and respected, and we want you to feel this too. We welcome new ideas, thinking and approaches, whilst listening to all our employees.

We're not looking for someone who's heard of AI. We're looking for someone who builds with it.

Forest One Australia is Australia's largest independent distributor of plywood, timber, laminate and decorative surface materials. We're Australian-owned, sustainability-focused, and currently in the middle of a genuine technology transformation — and we're looking for a developer who wants to be right in the middle of it.

This isn't a role where you'll be maintaining someone else's legacy code and waiting for tickets. You'll be building new things, using AI-assisted development as a core part of how you work, and delivering products that directly change how our customers and business operate.

What you'll build

We have a roadmap that includes a full rebuild of our eCommerce Trade Portal, an AI-powered delivery experience product, and a suite of internal tools that modernise how our business runs. You'll own the technical delivery of these initiatives as part of a small, senior team.

We use tools like Google AntiGravity and Claude for AI-assisted development — not as a gimmick, but because they genuinely make us faster and better. If that's already how you think about building software, you'll fit right in.

Someone who's genuinely curious, moves fast, and takes ownership

If you're the kind of developer who reads about new LLM capabilities on a Saturday morning because you're genuinely interested, we'd love to hear from you.

Job Title: AI Lead – Trading Systems & Agent Development
Ad Text:
Most AI systems never face reality.

Ours do.

We deploy into live trading environments where information is incomplete, conditions change quickly, and performance is measurable in real time.

We are looking for someone to build systems that improve how decisions are made and executed under pressure — systems trusted because they work, not because they sound impressive.

This role carries real ownership, direct commercial impact, and unusually fast feedback loops.

The Opportunity

You will work directly with traders and decision-makers to build AI systems that:
- identify actionable signals faster
- improve decision quality under uncertainty
- compress the time between insight and execution
- increase consistency and execution precision
- continuously improve from live outcomes

You will own systems end-to-end:
problem framing → architecture → deployment → iteration

The expectation is production impact.

What Makes This Role Different

Most AI roles sit far from commercial reality.

This one does not.

Your work will be deployed quickly, tested under live conditions, and measured against outcomes that matter.

You will operate with:
- direct access to decision-makers
- minimal bureaucracy
- high autonomy
- high accountability
- tight feedback loops
- meaningful commercial exposure

This is an environment for builders who want to see their systems used, trusted, and scaled.

What We’re Looking For

You have already built systems that operate reliably in production environments.

You are comfortable working with:
- incomplete information
- imperfect data
- unclear problem definitions
- fast iteration cycles
- high-stakes decision environments

You move quickly.
You think commercially.
You care about measurable outcomes.

You are likely stronger at shipping than presenting.

Technical Background

You will probably have strong experience across several of:
- Python and modern ML infrastructure
- LLM systems and agent architectures
- orchestration, retrieval, and evaluation systems
- reinforcement learning or optimisation systems
- event-driven or streaming systems
- low-latency production environments
- real-time inference infrastructure

Experience in trading, betting, or probabilistic decision systems is useful but not required.

We care more about judgement, speed, and the ability to build systems that survive contact with reality.

What Success Looks Like

Within the first few months, we would expect:
- multiple systems deployed into live workflows
- measurable improvements against baseline performance
- tighter decision loops
- increased execution consistency
- systems trusted by operators in production

Success in this role is measured by commercial impact.

Environment

- Small team
- High trust
- Fast execution
- Direct communication
- High standards
- Strong ownership culture

We hire carefully and prefer a small number of exceptional people over scaling headcount.

Compensation

Compensation is flexible and designed to attract exceptional individuals.

Data Engineer
NBL

The data behind the game matters as much as the game itself.

The NBL is one of the world's fastest-growing basketball leagues, and we're building the data infrastructure to match. This is a role for someone who thinks in systems, not just tasks, someone who finds genuine satisfaction in building things that last and cares as much about the business outcome as the elegance of the solution.

When this role is thriving, every team across NBL Group has clean, reliable data they can actually use. The decisions that shape fan experiences, commercial partnerships, and competition strategy are grounded in something they can trust. That's what you'll be building.

About Us

The National Basketball League is regarded as one of the premier basketball leagues in the world, home to some of the world's best players, and truly a sports entertainment experience like no other. We're a league that's growing fast, thinking big and constantly raising the bar. If you're passionate about sport and want to be part of something that's genuinely on the rise, you'll find your place here.

The Role

Sitting within the Fan Data and Analytics team and reporting to the Fan Data and Analytics Manager, you'll own the infrastructure that keeps data moving across the entire organisation.

Day to day, you'll be:

Building and maintaining scalable, secure data pipelines that collect and process data from ticketing, broadcast, digital platforms, and game sources
Managing and optimising the League's data warehouse so data is accurate, accessible, and ready for analysis at any time
Integrating diverse datasets spanning commercial, fan engagement, financial, and operational data into a single source of truth
Delivering real-time data solutions for live game insights as well as longer-term analysis of fan behaviour and commercial trends
Embedding automation across data collection, processing, and reporting to reduce manual effort and improve reliability
Maintaining data quality controls, governance frameworks, and compliance with privacy legislation
Collaborating with teams across Commercial, Marketing, Operations, Competition, and Finance to deliver data solutions that are actually useful
Managing relationships with technology vendors, clubs, and external partners to keep integrations running seamlessly
Exploring emerging technologies and translating industry trends in sports data and broadcast analytics into practical improvements
Who We're Looking For

You're the right person for this role if you read the responsibilities above and thought 'that's me.' More specifically, you bring:

Strong SQL skills and proven experience with both relational and non-relational databases
Solid experience with data warehousing solutions and data modelling techniques
Experience with big data and streaming concepts (e.g. event-driven architectures, real-time ingestion), implemented using Google Cloud tools such as BigQuery and Pub/Sub.
A working knowledge of data governance, privacy, and compliance requirements
The ability to communicate complex technical challenges clearly to non-technical stakeholders
Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Information Systems, or a related field
It's a bonus if you've worked in sport, entertainment, or consumer-facing industries, and if you have experience across large, complex multi-source datasets. Most of all, a genuine passion for sport and what good data does for the fan experience will make this role genuinely meaningful for you.

Our Values
  
These are not a poster on the wall. They are how we operate every day.
  
Always Think Big. Be courageous. Be open to new ideas. Learn and grow. No limits.
Be Accountable. Own it. Ask for help. The scoreboard matters.
Integrity is Key. Act ethically. Act honestly. Keep our word.
Show Respect. Show care. Listen and seek to understand. Value opinions.
We Are Fans Too. Have fun. Always consider the fan. Share the love of the game.
One Team. Communicate. Trust each other. Celebrate the wins. Build relationships.

What's on Offer

The chance to shape the data infrastructure of one of the world's fastest-growing basketball leagues
 Real cross-functional exposure across commercial, marketing, operations and competition
A team that is genuinely invested in building something great, not just maintaining the status quo
The opportunity to work at the intersection of sport and data, where your work has a direct impact on the fan experience
Ready to own it?
  
If you read this and it felt right, we'd love to hear from you. Apply with your CV and a brief note on what draws you to this role and the NBL.

ABOUT ROCKET LAB

Rocket Lab is an end-to-end space company delivering responsive launch services, complete spacecraft design and manufacturing, payloads, satellite components, and more - all with the goal of opening access space. The rockets and satellites we build, and launch enable some of the most ambitious and vital space missions globally, supporting scientific exploration, Earth observation and missions to combat climate change, national security, and exciting new technology demonstrations.

Our Electron rocket has become the second most frequently launched U.S. rocket annually and has delivered more than 230 satellites to orbit, all while we work to develop Neutron, our upcoming medium-lift, reusable launch vehicle for larger constellation deployment. Our Space Systems business designs and builds our extensive line of satellites, payloads, and their components, including spacecraft that have been selected to support NASA missions to the Moon and Mars and components used on the James Webb Space Telescope.



ELECTRON
Rocket Lab's Electron team is responsible for building, testing, and launching our Electron rocket across our launch sites and test facilities in the United States and New Zealand. Electron is one of the world's most frequently-launched rockets, and our team behind its success are diligent, move fast, and have each others' back to deliver a reliable rocket to the launch pad time and time again.


About the role

Based in Auckland, NZ, the Senior HITL Engineer is responsible for designing, maintaining, and operating hardware in the loop (HITL) systems. You will support the Production, Electron, and Neutron organizations, with deliverables required from the business leadership team.

WHAT YOU'LL GET TO DO:


Work within a small team dedicated to designing, operating, and maintaining the avionic test systems to support all of Rocket Lab's mission from the production floor to the launch operations.
Ensure the HITL systems are subjected to an appropriate level of engineering rigor and documentation.
Work with other teams to verify the functionality and performance of the Rocket Lab avionics components.
Operate launch vehicle simulation platform for Day of Launch operations for specific missions.
Work hands-on with test and flight space hardware.
Make and maintain accurate technical documentation via system diagrams, schematics, ICDs, procedures, test/anomaly reports.
Analyze test results from raw data for pass/fail criteria.
Troubleshoot root cause from anomalies and communicate the results and path forward with internal teams, vendors, and customers.
Operate and capture data from test equipment (DMMs, oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, spectrum analyzers, etc.).
Prioritize, manage, and drive multiple projects at the same time.
Work on the HITL standardization processes and automation.
Provide technical expertise and training for the more junior HITL team members.
YOU'LL BRING THESE QUALIFICATIONS:


Bachelor's degree in mechatronics, electronics, computer science, aerospace engineering, or related field.
8+ years of engineering experience with hardware or software development.
Experience with designing and implementing electronics test systems.
Experience writing rigorous test procedures.
Meticulous with configuration management and documentation.
Experience with designing and assembling test racks.
THESE QUALIFICATIONS WOULD BE NICE TO HAVE:


Master's or PhD in mechatronics, electronics, computer science, aerospace engineering, or related field.
Prior experience working on a HITL test, Avionics test, or Software test team.
Ability to understand and create technical documentation via system diagrams, schematics, ICDs, procedures, test/anomaly reports.
Prior experience participating to design and acceptance reviews.
Theoretical and practical understanding of electronic systems.
Strong troubleshooting skills and an aptitude for technical guidance and mentoring of junior engineers.
Ability to work under pressure and adhere to tight deadlines.
Have the mindset of actively make and break to find flaws and issues and report them.
Experience with harness manufacturing, electrical connectors, crimping, and soldering.
Familiarity with Ethernet, RS485/422/232, CAN, LVDS, SpaceWire protocols.
Strong understanding of sensors, signal conditioning, and data acquisition electronics.
Experience with computer networking: switching, routing and subnetting.
Familiarity with CAD for test fixture and support equipment layout/design.
Experience reading engineering drawings.
Experience with PLCs, relays, and power systems.
Familiarity with Python, C++, C#, Linux, CI/CD, Git, NX and SolidWorks.
Knowledge of spacecraft systems or similar highly complex and autonomous systems.
Experience with general design, documentation, and presentation in Microsoft Suite (Teams, Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
Experience with quality reporting and management, work instruction peer review, test procedure or data review, and time recording in Jira.
Experience supporting production engineers.
Prior project management experience, juggling multiple timelines and milestones.
Ability to be self-motivated.

WHAT TO EXPECT

We're on a mission to unlock the potential of space to improve life on Earth, but that's not an easy task. It takes hard work, determination, relentless innovation, teamwork, grit, and an unwavering commitment to achieving what others often deem impossible. Our people out-think, out-work and out-pace. We pride ourselves on having each other's backs, checking our egos at the door, and rolling up our sleeves on all tasks big and small. We thrive under pressure, work to tight deadlines, and our focus is always on how we can deliver, rather than dwelling on the challenges that stand in the way.

What Kestral is all about 

Kestral is passionate about creating the industry standard in Radiology and Pathology information technology. We provide innovative software solutions that support laboratories and clinicians across Australia to deliver accurate, timely healthcare outcomes. 

At Kestral, we thrive on working with talented, motivated people who enjoy solving problems, improving systems, and making a genuine impact through technology. We’re a collaborative, high‑performing team with a shared commitment to quality, innovation, and customer outcomes. 

Join us and be part of a team that is genuinely proud of what we build and the difference it makes in healthcare. 




What you’ll be doing at Kestral 

We are seeking a Pathology Systems Engineer to join our Pathology Laboratory Systems (PLS) team. This role plays a critical part in supporting, configuring, integrating, and enhancing our pathology software solutions for new and existing customers. 

You’ll work closely with laboratory and IT teams, developing technical solutions that support laboratory workflows, system integrations, reporting, and automation—while providing high‑quality customer support and continuous improvement. 




Key Responsibilities 

Assist with the installation, configuration, and ongoing support of Kestral pathology systems for customer laboratories 

Design, develop, test, deploy, and maintain software scripts, report definitions (RDLs), and system configurations that underpin:  

Laboratory reporting 

Instrument interfacing and data integration 

HL7 message transformations and interoperability 

Diagnose and resolve complex system, integration, and data issues across development, test, and production environments 

Work directly with customers to understand laboratory workflows, identify system limitations, and recommend improvements or automation opportunities 

Conduct site visits to observe workflows, engage with laboratory and IT staff, and deliver practical, fit‑for‑purpose solutions 

Provide proactive customer and account support, including logging and managing support requests, upgrades, and enhancements 

Contribute to successful system upgrades, installations, extensions, and post go‑live support 

Develop and maintain technical and training documentation, and deliver training to customers and internal staff as required 

Collaborate with colleagues across Kestral on technical issues, projects, and continuous improvement initiatives 

Explore and evaluate emerging technologies, including AI‑driven tools and modern healthcare integration standards, to support innovation within pathology systems 




What we’re looking for 

Skills & Experience 

Australian recognised qualifications in IT and experience working with computer systems and supporting information systems 

Demonstrated technical aptitude, with proven ability to develop, test, deploy, and maintain scripts and software configurations across laboratory reporting, instrument integration, data transformation systems and AI‑driven analytical tools 

Proven ability to diagnose and resolve software defects, system errors, interface failures, and integration issues by tracing problems through multiple system layers, including undertaking software testing prior to customer delivery 

Capability to assess complex laboratory workflows through direct customer engagement and observation, and convert findings into documented business requirements, improvement recommendations, and implemented technical solutions 

Ability to identify manual or repetitive processes within laboratory workflows and develop scripted or automated solutions to improve efficiency and reduce errors 

Experience working with healthcare or complex integrated systems, preferably within pathology or radiology environments 

Experience working with healthcare interoperability standards, including HL7 and FHIR, and building or maintaining message transformation scripts and integration mappings 

Experience providing customer and product support, including building and maintaining effective customer relationships and delivering outcomes that support customer satisfaction 

Experience developing, maintaining, and delivering training materials and documentation, and providing training to customers and internal staff 

Professional and effective written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to liaise effectively with customers, users, and colleagues across all parts of the business 

Ability to work effectively as part of a team or independently when required, including collaborating with colleagues to meet shared objectives 

Ability to manage workload through effective prioritisation, multitasking, and keeping relevant stakeholders informed of progress 

High level of self‑motivation, professionalism, integrity, and maturity in presentation, attitude, and behaviour 

Knowledge of Australian healthcare regulatory frameworks, including NATA, TGA, RCPA, and Medicare  




Want to get started? 

If this role sounds like a great fit for you, we’d love to hear from you. Please submit your CV and an optional cover letter outlining your experience and interest in joining the Kestral team. 

Kestral is a subsidiary of Jonas Software, a leading provider of enterprise management software for more than 40,000 customers across 15 countries. Jonas Software is a wholly owned subsidiary of Constellation Software Inc. (TSX: CSU).

Valocity overview

At Valocity, we build the digital infrastructure that powers confident property decisions around the world.

Our products sit at the centre of how property is valued, bought, sold, financed, and insured. We combine decision-critical data, AI-driven insights, and workflow-embedded software to help professionals make better decisions, faster, and with greater confidence.

Valocity is used across more than 4,000 cities globally, supporting lenders, valuers, brokers, insurers, real estate agents, and enterprise partners across multiple markets and regulatory environments. We are not building one-off tools. We are building platforms our customers rely on every day to run their businesses.

Our innovation and impact have been on the global stage, including:

Winner, Best International Fintech, India Fintech Forum 2026

Runner up, Global fintech of the year, Singapore Fintech Festival 2022

With teams across New Zealand, Australia, and India, Valocity is growing fast and thinking long-term. Design is central to that growth. It is how we simplify complex problems, create trust at scale, and deliver consistent, high-quality experiences across every market we serve.

Valocity Australia is seeking a full-time Solutions and Insights Specialist to support its growing data solutions and platform integration operations. The role focuses on analysing business needs, translating them into system requirements, and supporting the implementation of data-driven solutions across the organisation. The position works closely with stakeholders across product, engineering, analytics, and client-facing teams to ensure effective solution delivery and continuous system improvement.

What You’ll be Responsible For:

·        Engage with internal and external stakeholders to elicit, analyse, and document business requirements, system needs, and functional objectives

·        Analyse existing business processes, operational workflows, and system integrations to identify inefficiencies and recommend improvements

·        Translate business requirements into detailed functional specifications, user stories, and technical documentation for product and engineering teams

·        Conduct feasibility studies, gap analysis, and impact assessments for system enhancements, data integrations, and process improvements

·        Facilitate stakeholder workshops, interviews, and requirement validation sessions to ensure accuracy, completeness, and alignment of requirements

·        Act as a liaison between business stakeholders and technical teams to ensure system solutions align with business objectives and operational needs

·        Support the design and implementation of system and data solutions, ensuring alignment with defined requirements and organisational standards

·        Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including product, engineering, and analytics, to ensure effective delivery of system solutions

·        Assist in system testing activities, including user acceptance testing (UAT), to validate that implemented solutions meet business and functional requirements

·        Develop and maintain documentation such as business requirement documents (BRDs), functional specifications, process maps, and workflow diagrams

·        Monitor system performance, user adoption, and stakeholder feedback to identify opportunities for continuous improvement

·        Provide recommendations on system enhancements, process optimisation, and technology improvements to support business operations and decision-making

Required Skills and Experience:

·        Bachelor’s degree in IT, Computer Science, Data Analytics, or a related field

·        Minimum 3 years of relevant experience in business analysis or systems analysis

·        Demonstrated experience in requirements gathering, process mapping, and system analysis

·        Experience working with cross-functional teams including product and engineering

·        Exposure to data visualisation tools (e.g. Power BI, Tableau) and basic SQL

·        Strong analytical, problem-solving, and stakeholder communication skills

What We Offer:

·        Competitive salary package aligned with market standards

·        Opportunity to work in a high-growth proptech and data-driven environment

·        Exposure to cutting-edge data, analytics, and platform solutions across the property and financial services sector

·        Collaborative and supportive team environment with cross-functional learning opportunities

·        Long-term career growth in business analysis, data solutions, and technology consulting

Employer questions

Your application will include the following questions:

·        What is your current work authorisation status in Australia?

·        Do you have at least 3+ years of experience in business analysis, data analytics, or a technology-related role?

·        Do you have experience working with cross-functional teams (e.g. product, engineering, analytics)?

·        Do you have experience in gathering and documenting business and system requirements?

·        Do you hold a relevant qualification in IT, Computer Science, Data Analytics, or a related field?

Interested?

Due to the high volume of applications, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Please apply through the platform and ensure your resume is up to date.

Australian Citizens and Permanent Residents are encouraged to apply.

Boeing Global Technology - Australia (BGT-A) supports the future growth and profitability of Boeing Australia's businesses and the broader Boeing enterprise. This advanced research and development arm creates future aerospace solutions while improving the cycle time, cost, quality and performance of current systems.

This role is based in Brisbane City and St Lucia, QLD.

As a Senior Systems Engineer (Autonomy), you will:

Apply systems thinking to model, integrate, verify and validate interoperable autonomy services within the autonomous system architecture. These may include flight autonomy, mission autonomy, mission systems, C2 environments, devops and operational testing.
Coordinate cross-functional and business unit stakeholders to capture the technical and non-technical data required across the systems engineering lifecycle.
Employ best practice in Model-Based Systems Engineering and Mission Engineering to develop, interrogate and export system model descriptions that are clear and reviewable for cross-functional stakeholders.
Design performance measures at different levels of the system hierarchy that reflect impact on the overall system, and the aggregation of sub-system measures into overall system performance.
Develop and execute verification and validation test plans using simulation and flight tests.
Make recommendations that support decisions under risk and under extreme uncertainty; adapt with agility as the situations evolve.
Extend the state of the art in aerospace systems design to support functional allocation of traditionally human-executed functions between human and autonomy components of the system.

About you

To be successful in this role you will have:

Required:

Tertiary qualification in systems engineering, other engineering disciplines (including aerospace, mechatronics, electrical, or software), computer science, physics or related disciplines.
Five (5) or more years of experience in professional engineering or research roles (or equivalent combination of higher degree and professional experience).
Professional experience in Systems Engineering.
Ability to work effectively in ambiguous, fast-changing technical environments.
Ability to work both as an independent contributor, and as an active collaborator within a cross-disciplinary team.
Be an Australian citizen and hold, or fulfill the requirements to obtain a NV-1 Australian Defence security clearance.

Desirable:

Experience in Model-Based Systems Engineering and SysML.
Experience in tools, including Dassault MSOSA and devops test pipelines.
Demonstrated experience in autonomous systems or robotics applications.
Demonstrated systems engineering experience for the earlier developmental / prototyping stages of technology.

At CI&T, we're redefining the future of digital experiences with the power of AI technology. We are on the lookout for a highly technical, AI-forward professional to drive a major legacy migration and modernisation project, combining the best of business analysis and automation quality assurance.

About the Role:

As an AI-Assisted Modernisation Engineer, you will leverage advanced AI tools, including large language models (LLMs) and autonomous agents, to transform traditional manual analysis and testing into a streamlined, automated process. Your primary focus will be on reverse-engineering requirements from our existing codebase and executing automated QA test cases, ensuring the highest standards of quality and performance for modernised systems.

This is an unique position combining the strategic insights of a Business Analyst with the precision and quality assurance of an Automation QA. 

Key Responsibilities:

AI-Driven Business Analysis: Deploy and orchestrate AI agents to analyse legacy codebases, extracting business logic and generating comprehensive, modern requirement documents through reverse engineering.
AI-Driven Quality Assurance: Design prompts and agent workflows that automatically generate, execute, and validate test cases against the modernised system, ensuring robust quality checks.
Prompt Engineering & Orchestration: Refine LLM prompts and manage agent frameworks to ensure high-accuracy outputs, minimising AI hallucinations in both requirements and test results.
Validation & Human-in-the-Loop: Act as the critical bridge between AI output and reality, manually validating generated requirements and test coverage to ensure alignment with business objectives.
Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams, including developers, Scrum Masters, and QA specialists, to ensure seamless integration of AI-driven processes into the software development lifecycle.

Who You Are:

SDLC Master: Deep understanding of software development, requirements elicitation, and QA methodologies.
AI/LLM Proficiency: Hands-on experience using LLMs (e.g., GPT-4, Claude 3.5) for coding, analysis, or automation tasks. Familiarity with agentic frameworks (e.g., LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI) is a significant advantage.
Code Literate: Ability to read and understand complex code structures in languages such as Java, C#, Python, or others relevant to legacy and modern systems.
QA Automation Expert: Familiarity with test automation principles and integration of AI-generated tests into continuous integration pipelines.
Critical Thinker: Exceptional critical thinking skills to validate AI outputs and ensure comprehensive test coverage.

Company Description


Bilue is a digital consultancy that designs and builds smart, user-friendly technology for some of Australia’s most well-known businesses. From mobile apps to beautifully designed web platforms and digital experiences, we create solutions that drive impact and deliver exceptional customer outcomes.

Our culture is people-first and purpose-driven. We’re a down-to-earth, values-led team with offices in Sydney and Melbourne, and a growing presence in Manila. We genuinely enjoy working together, whether we’re solving tough tech problems, brainstorming creative solutions, or grabbing a coffee between meetings. Curiosity is encouraged. Collaboration is second nature.

We value excellence, not ego, and back each other to do great work without micromanagement. With low politics and high trust, it’s a place where delivery people, designers, and engineers genuinely connect, and where everyone has a voice, space to grow, and a little fun along the way.

Job Description


The Role

AI systems don’t fail because of bad models. They fail because of bad libraries, outdated documents cited as current, knowledge that exists but can’t be found, datasets that are technically present but practically untrustworthy.

The AI Information Scientist owns that problem. This is not a traditional data engineering role. It sits at the intersection of data governance, knowledge management, and AI delivery. You will design and maintain the catalogues, metadata schemas, quality frameworks, and lineage structures that every AI system in The Foundry depends on, and you will work directly alongside AI Engineers to connect retrieval systems to the clean, context-aware knowledge stores you’ve built.

Think of it this way: the AI Engineer builds the system that searches the library. You build the library.

What You'll Do

Design and maintain data catalogues for AI projects using platforms such as DataHub, OpenMetadata, Apache Atlas, Collibra, or cloud-native equivalents (AWS Glue Data Catalog, Azure Purview, GCP Dataplex).

Define metadata schemas and taxonomy standards — type, version, jurisdiction, validity period, confidence tier — so retrieval systems know not just what a document is, but when it applies and how much to trust it.

Assess data quality across client and internal assets using tools like Great Expectations, dbt tests, or Soda; flag stale, superseded, or ambiguous records before they reach the AI layer.

Build and maintain data lineage so every AI-generated output can be traced back to its source, version, and validity period — making outputs auditable, not just accurate.

Design automated ingestion workflows and nightly quality checks that keep catalogues current without constant manual intervention.

Partner with The Foundry engineers to connect RAG pipelines and agentic retrieval systems to catalogue APIs, and with The Labs strategists to map client data estates before a project begins.

Qualifications


What We're Looking For

A background in data governance, information management, knowledge management, or records management — with genuine interest in how that work enables AI delivery.

Hands-on experience with at least one data catalogue platform (DataHub, OpenMetadata, Collibra, Alation, Apache Atlas, or a major cloud equivalent) and familiarity with metadata standards such as JSON-LD, Dublin Core, or domain-specific ontologies.

Strong SQL skills; working Python for data profiling, quality scripting, and metadata automation; and comfort with dbt or OpenLineage for lineage tracking.

Understanding of vector databases and RAG architecture — enough to know how metadata quality directly affects retrieval precision.

Experience in regulated or high-stakes data environments where provenance and auditability genuinely matter: financial services, insurance, government, healthcare, or similar.

A collaborative, low-ego disposition. The Data Librarian’s work is structural and often invisible. The glory goes to the AI system. You are fine with that.


Bonus: A formal background in library or information science. Experience with knowledge graphs (Neo4j, RDF, SPARQL). Prior consulting or agency delivery experience.

Additional Information


Life at Bilue

People-first focus: We’re committed to delivering exceptional outcomes for our clients, but we know it starts with our people. You’ll join a values-led team that’s collaborative, curious, and genuinely cares about doing great work, together.

Connection that counts: From monthly anchor days and team lunches to our annual offsite, we create intentional moments to connect, collaborate, and celebrate. These aren’t just fun perks — they’re part of how we work and grow together.

Flexibility that works: We offer hybrid working, with 1–2 days per week in the office. It’s a balance that gives you the space to do your best work, while still creating time to connect and build strong relationships in person.

Strong internal communities: We actively foster internal communities across tech, design, delivery, and beyond, giving you plenty of chances to connect, share knowledge, and learn from your peers.

Opportunities to grow: We invest in your development with unlimited access to Go1’s learning library and support from our internal performance coach. Whether you want to deepen your technical skills or grow your leadership potential, we’ll back you.

Flat structure, real impact: At Bilue, everyone’s voice matters. Our leadership team is hands-on and approachable, and we operate without unnecessary layers. We keep things open and transparent, and your ideas will be heard, no matter your title.

Bilue = Big + Blue Ocean. Are you ready to set sail? Apply now!

NB. This is a full-time position based in Sydney, NSW or Melbourne, VIC. To be considered, candidates must have unrestricted working rights in Australia.
